Scenario B — Over-Privileged Role Analysis
Recommendation: Remove unused entitlements detected by Zendata telemetry for members of this role.
Scenario F — Machine Agent Entitlement Usage
Recommendation: Machine agents should operate under strict least-privilege. Entitlements showing zero usage should be immediately revoked.
Scenario G — Agent Scope Creep
Data Source Access by Phase
Risk Score Progression (Monthly)
Agent data access monitored longitudinally over phases. Sudden expansions in accessed datasources without formal recertification indicate scope drift.

Machine / Service Agent Inventory
WHY MACHINE IDENTITIES MATTER FOR IAM
IAM traditionally governs human identity provisioning and lifecycle. Machine identities — service accounts, API keys, AI agents — are provisioned once and rarely reviewed. While IAM assigns low static risk, Zendata behavioral analytics tracks their actual telemetry over time to identify scope drift, PII mishandling, and cross-department privilege escalation.

The feedback loop: Zendata signals trigger IAM access certifications dynamically based on behavioral anomalies.
